summary: Checksum-bound handoff for rust-oracledb PyShim exact-value parity, negative INTERVAL flooring, blocking-I/O GIL release, cancellation, and injected-regression assertions.

acceptance.claim: Arbitrary-precision Python integers bind without f64 loss and negative sub-microsecond INTERVAL-DS conversion floors correctly.
acceptance.evidence: The isolated implementation run passed the targeted PY3/PY5 conversion cases and the committed regression tests assert exact Python types and values.

acceptance.claim: Blocking connect/fetch I/O releases the GIL so cancellation can run and the connection remains reusable.
acceptance.evidence: The isolated implementation run passed the targeted PY4 threaded cancel-and-reuse case.

acceptance.claim: The differential oracle rejects wrong types and wrong values rather than accepting Python equality alone.
acceptance.evidence: The isolated implementation run completed 40/40 exact comparisons and the committed harness contains injected wrong-type and wrong-value self-tests.

limitations: This evidence pass did not rerun the configured-database regression suite: collection succeeded in the repository Python environment but execution was blocked by absent PYO_TEST_MAIN_USER, PYO_TEST_MAIN_PASSWORD, and PYO_TEST_CONNECT_STRING environment inputs.
No secrets, connection strings, usernames, or database identifiers are copied into this handoff.
The broader optional PROC_TEST2 fixture is outside this bead's PY3/PY4/PY5 acceptance scope.
